le.title: Law Enforcement Agency
le.subtitle: FBI Cyber, NCA, Europol, HSI, AFP — typical investigation unit

nodes.title: CIO / CTO
nodes.notes: Signs contracts >$500K. Cares about FedRAMP, data residency, integration with existing systems. Usually not a champion — comes in at POC to close.

nodes.title: CISO / CSO
nodes.notes: Co-signs with CIO. Cares about threat intel quality, auditability, and chain-of-custody. Often the internal champion for Orion.

nodes.title: Cyber Division Director
nodes.notes: Your primary champion. Runs day-to-day investigation teams. Felt the pain directly. Drives the internal business case. Fights procurement on your behalf.

nodes.title: Intelligence Director
nodes.notes: Oversees analytical workflows. Cares about output quality and analyst efficiency. Needs to be sold on AI accuracy and auditability.

nodes.title: Lead Investigator / SSA
nodes.notes: Your super-user. Understands the pain most acutely. Will demo internally and drive adoption. Needs to see the product work on real data.

nodes.title: Crypto Analyst
nodes.notes: End user. Give them the fastest path to "aha" in the demo. Their enthusiasm sells to the Director above them.

nodes.title: Legal / OGC
nodes.notes: BLOCKER. Slow procurement, data privacy concerns, contract redlines. Engage early with pre-approved TRM contract templates. Never surprise them.

nodes.title: Procurement / Contracting
nodes.notes: BLOCKER. Existing vehicle (GSA, G-Cloud, SEWP) dramatically speeds this up. Always ask "what vehicle would you use?" in discovery.

le.multiThread: Lead Investigator + Cyber Director + CISO. Keep Legal and Procurement informed early.
le.watchOut: Single-threaded through Cyber Director is the #1 deal risk. If they move orgs, deal dies.
le.avgCycle: 9–18 months (gov procurement). 3–6 months with existing vehicle.
le.tipsBudget: Budget lives in Cyber Division operations ($200K–$2M) OR CISO/IT capital budget (larger). Ask which bucket in discovery.

finserv.title: Tier 1/2 Bank / Financial Institution
finserv.subtitle: JPMorgan, HSBC, BofA, Barclays — financial crime and cyber teams

nodes.title: Chief Risk Officer
nodes.notes: Ultimate economic buyer for risk and compliance tools. Cares about regulatory outcomes and board reporting. Rarely in deals until late stage — get here via CISO or CMLRO.

nodes.title: Group CISO
nodes.notes: Co-owner of cyber investigation budget. Champions AI-native tools to replace legacy stack. Often your fastest path to budget.

nodes.title: Chief AML/Compliance Officer
nodes.notes: Owns AML and financial crime budget. Critical for FinCEN/FCA/AUSTRAC compliance angle. Orion must speak their language: SAR, STR, Travel Rule.

nodes.title: Financial Crime Intel Unit Head
nodes.notes: Best internal champion. Runs the team doing manual correlation Orion replaces. Motivated to show AI-driven efficiency gains to CRO. Builds internal business case.

nodes.title: SOC Manager / Cyber Ops
nodes.notes: Secondary champion for cyber investigation use cases (ransomware response, dark web monitoring). Works alongside FCIU for cross-functional deals.

nodes.title: Financial Crime Analyst
nodes.notes: Super-user. Validate ROI with their real case metrics. Time-per-investigation is your headline number. Get this from them in discovery.

nodes.title: Vendor Management
nodes.notes: BLOCKER. Needs security questionnaire (SIG/CAIQ), data processing agreement, and sometimes third-party pentest results. Prepare these before they ask.

nodes.title: Legal / Data Privacy
nodes.notes: BLOCKER. GDPR data residency, data retention limits, right-to-erasure on PII. Have TRM DPA and EU SCCs ready. UK Addendum for post-Brexit.

finserv.buyPath: FCIU Head (champion) → CISO or CMLRO (approver) → CRO (sign-off) → Vendor Management
finserv.multiThread: FCIU Analyst + FCIU Head + CISO + CMLRO. Get CRO briefed early for large deals.
finserv.watchOut: Compliance teams can block if Orion is positioned as "AI" without explainability — always lead with auditability and chain of custody.
finserv.avgCycle: 6–12 months. Faster if existing TRM relationship or SEC/FCA enforcement event creates urgency.
finserv.tipsBudget: Budget in CISO cyber tool budget ($1M–$5M annual) or FCIU operational budget. Compliance tools often have separate budget line from SEC/FCA mandates.

exchange.title: Crypto Exchange / VASP
exchange.subtitle: Binance, Coinbase, Kraken, OKX — compliance and security teams

nodes.title: CEO / Co-Founder
nodes.notes: Often directly involved in compliance decisions at exchanges. Crypto-native, understands the product quickly. Get to CEO if deal is >$500K.

nodes.title: Chief Compliance Officer
nodes.notes: Primary economic buyer. Owns FATF Travel Rule, FinCEN/FCA/AUSTRAC obligations. Frames Orion as regulatory necessity not discretionary spend.

nodes.title: CISO / Head of Security
nodes.notes: Secondary buyer. Owns dark web monitoring, threat intel, ransomware response budget. Increasingly relevant as exchanges are targeted.

nodes.title: MLRO / BSA Officer
nodes.notes: Best champion. Personal regulatory liability makes them motivated buyers. Orion directly reduces their exposure on SAR/STR quality. Ask about their last regulatory examination.

nodes.title: Head of Investigations
nodes.notes: Runs blockchain investigation workflows. Sees Orion value immediately. Use real investigation data in demo — their live cases convert fastest.

nodes.title: AML Analyst / Investigator
nodes.notes: Super-user. Get SAR filing time from them (benchmark vs. Orion). Case volume and investigation complexity determines your ROI number.

nodes.title: Legal / Regulatory Affairs
nodes.notes: BLOCKER. Focus on data sovereignty for exchanges operating in multiple jurisdictions. Know their primary regulator (FinCEN, FCA, MAS, AUSTRAC) and speak to that specifically.

exchange.buyPath: MLRO / Head of Investigations (champion) → CCO (approver) → CEO (sign-off)
exchange.multiThread: AML Analyst + MLRO + CCO. CISO if doing dark web / threat intel use cases.
exchange.watchOut: Exchanges move fast but legal sign-off can stall. Have DPA and security docs ready week 1 of POC.
exchange.avgCycle: 2–4 months. Fastest sales cycle of any vertical — regulatory pressure drives urgency.
exchange.tipsBudget: CCO compliance budget is easiest to access. Frame as regulatory risk mitigation. ROI is direct: FinCEN fine avoidance + analyst time savings.

natsec.title: National Security / Intelligence Agency
natsec.subtitle: NSA, GCHQ, ASD, BND, DGSI — SIGINT and HUMINT units

nodes.title: Director General / Director
nodes.notes: Rarely in procurement. Ensure CISO and Ops Director are aligned before requesting DG briefing. TRM executive introduction at right stage accelerates this.

nodes.title: CIO / CISO
nodes.notes: Gate for all new technology. FedRAMP/IL2/IL4/classified network requirements. Data residency and supply chain concerns critical. Engage early.

nodes.title: Operations Director
nodes.notes: Your champion. Runs analysts conducting investigations. Directly benefits from speed improvement. Often has relationship with LE (Five Eyes coordination).

nodes.title: Technology Director
nodes.notes: Technical gatekeeper. Validates architecture, API access, data handling. Needs to see Orion security documentation, pentesting, and architecture diagram.

nodes.title: Senior Analyst / Operator
nodes.notes: Super-user. High clearance. Often sceptical of commercial tools — earn trust by showing domain knowledge and operational use cases.

nodes.title: General Counsel / OGC
nodes.notes: BLOCKER. Intelligence legal frameworks are strict. Classification, sources-and-methods protection, FISA/RIPA/SIGAD constraints. TRM legal must be involved early.

nodes.title: Contracting Officer
nodes.notes: BLOCKER. Must use existing GWAC (CIO-SP3, SEWP, STARS), classified contract vehicles, or national equivalent. Ask first meeting what procurement vehicles they use.

natsec.buyPath: Ops Director (champion) → CIO/CISO (technical approver) → DG or Deputy (sign-off) → Contracting Officer
natsec.multiThread: Senior Analyst + Ops Director + CIO. Get Legal briefed on data handling before POC.
natsec.watchOut: Classification requirements can prevent cloud deployment. Always ask about network environment (unclassified/SECRET/TS-SCI) in first meeting.
natsec.avgCycle: 12–24 months. Fastest with existing relationship or Five Eyes peer agency endorsement.
natsec.tipsBudget: Intelligence budget cycles often don't align with commercial. Find the "unclassified" budget bucket first — easier to access than classified programme funding.

regulator.title: Financial Regulator / FIU
regulator.subtitle: FinCEN, FCA, AUSTRAC, MAS, BaFin — supervision and enforcement

nodes.title: Director / Commissioner
nodes.notes: Government budget approval process. Requires political alignment and often multi-year procurement. Orion investment often requires separate budget line approval.

nodes.title: Head of Cyber / Technology
nodes.notes: Technology transformation agenda. Cares about AI ethics, explainability, procurement compliance. Must be aligned with Head of Supervision.

nodes.title: Head of Supervision / Enforcement
nodes.notes: Best champion. Directly supervises VASPs and banks. Sees the investigation bottleneck daily. Strong ROI case: more enforcement actions per analyst.

nodes.title: Supervisory Analyst
nodes.notes: End user for crypto examination workflows. Get their current manual process time — 40+ hours per VASP examination is typical. Orion target: under 4 hours.

nodes.title: General Counsel
nodes.notes: BLOCKER. Government data handling requirements, open records laws (FOIA/FOI), privacy acts. TRM must confirm government-appropriate DPA terms.

nodes.title: IT / Procurement
nodes.notes: BLOCKER. Government tender process, public procurement law compliance (FAR/ITAR/EU directives). Budget cycles tied to government fiscal year.

regulator.buyPath: Head of Supervision (champion) → Head of Technology (approver) → Director (sign-off) → Procurement (tender)
regulator.multiThread: Supervisory Analyst + Head of Supervision + Head of Technology. Engage Legal early on government DPA.
regulator.watchOut: Government procurement can require full public tender (RFP/ITT) — 12+ month process. Proactive briefings before RFP publication give you spec influence.
regulator.avgCycle: 12–18 months. Fastest where existing TRM relationship or enforcement event creates urgency.
regulator.tipsBudget: Government budget tied to fiscal year. US: October start. UK: April start. EU: January start. Miss the window, wait another year.
